calendar.matters:
Petition For Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Successor Trustees, For Modification Of Trust To Provide For Appointment Of Non-Professional Fiduciaries As Trustees, Or In The Alternative For Termination Of Trust And For Distribution To Beneficiary
Off Calendar May-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for and with Lori Liuzzi (Petitioner).
Vincent Quinn also present.

Ms. Callejo stated that Ms. Witherspoon sent a message two weeks ago admitting to having taken $104,000.00 of the $226,000.00 she had collected, that Ms. Witherspoon was not bonded, that the Trust was not under Court supervision, and that her Client and the Client's Son were able to get records from Wells Fargo, but they are not complete records. Ms. Callejo also stated that Ms. Witherspoon stated that she is in the process of selling her condominium, that she had reached out to Ms. Witherspoon's Father, but the Father indicated that she is not responding to him, that she discussed with her Client that they may have to take other avenues in another arena against Ms. Witherspoon, but her Client is sympathetic to Ms. Witherspoon's situation and does not want to do that, and that Ms. Witherspoon is promising to pay things back. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that she spoke with the Realtor, that the Realtor stated that the condo is going to be listed, that her Client is really loathed to bringing in a Professional, that at this point she does not see any Professional wanting to come in and that it is not cost effective to have a Professional. Furthermore, Ms. Callejo stated that they are asking that her Client's Son be appointed, that there is nothing in this trust, that the statements show negative balances, read Ms. Witherspoon's e-mail to the Court, stated that Ms. Witherspoon started taking money in 2016 and expressed concern about other matters that Ms. Witherspoon may have taken money from. The Court expressed concern stating that the Trustor intended that a Professional be appointed and stated that this is going to be complicated. Ms. Callejo expressed concern about her fees, spoke of her Client's medical needs, spoke of perhaps trying to secure a form of judgment and stated that ordering an accounting would probably not be useful at this point as Ms. Witherspoon had indicated in February that she was going to provide one and still hasn't. The Court granted the Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order). Christine Witherspoon is removed. The Court appoints Vincent Quinn and Cullen Quinn as Co-Trustees without bond, but as soon as something is recovered they will have to petition to have a bond posted. The Court is not going to terminate the trust at this point or make any orders regarding fees at this point. All other relief requested is denied without prejudice to bringing appropriate petitions. Ms. Callejo will file a supplemental declaration attaching a copy of the e-mail she received from Ms. Witherspoon.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Exparte Petition For Suspension Of Trustee Christine V Witherspoon And For Appointment Of Vincent Quinn And Cullen Quinn As Temporary Successor Trustees Without Bond
Off Calendar Mar-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
